description: Code review validation - identify TDD issues in code samples

samples.label: Test testing implementation details
expected_findings: Couples test to internal data structure

samples.label: Test with no assertion
samples.code:
def test_process_order():
    service = OrderService(MockRepo())
    service.process(Order(items=[Item('Widget', 10)]))
    # if no exception, it works!

samples.label: Well-structured test with AAA
samples.code:
def test_applies_percentage_discount():
    # Arrange
    order = Order(items=[Item('Widget', Decimal('100.00'))])
    discount = PercentageDiscount(Decimal('10'))

    # Act
    discounted_order = discount.apply(order)

    # Assert
    assert discounted_order.total == Decimal('90.00')

samples.label: Over-mocked test
samples.code:
def test_create_invoice():
    mock_repo = Mock()
    mock_tax = Mock(return_value=Decimal('8.00'))
    mock_formatter = Mock(return_value='INV-001')
    mock_validator = Mock(return_value=True)
    mock_logger = Mock()
    mock_notifier = Mock()
    service = InvoiceService(mock_repo, mock_tax, mock_formatter, mock_validator, mock_logger, mock_notifier)
    service.create(order)
    mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()
expected_findings: Testing mock interactions rather than behavior
Hard to understand what is being tested
